title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants two defense-in-depth changes: (1) Add a second `XValidation` CEL rule on `PolicyOverrides` to reject empty rule-name keys (`""`) at the CRD schema layer, since the existing CEL only checks that rules/configMapRef are present and the webhook's empty-key rejection (REQ-008) can be bypassed if the webhook is unreachable. (2) Add a `maxActiveKeys < 3` guard in the webhook's `validate()` function to match the existing `+kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=3` CRD marker, mirroring how `Replicas` already has both a CRD marker and a defense-in-depth runtime check.

code_comments.body:
**CRD XValidation doesn't cover empty rule name constraint (REQ-008 gap)**

The `x-kubernetes-validations` rule guards only the *source-presence* requirement (`size(self.rules) > 0 || self.configMapRef != null`), but it doesn't prevent the empty-key rule that the webhook enforces in REQ-008 (`keystone_webhook.go:123-131`).

If the webhook server is unreachable, a payload like:

```yaml
policyOverrides:
  rules:
    "": "role:admin"
```

will **pass** the CEL rule (`size(self.rules) == 1 > 0`) and be persisted to etcd, even though the webhook would normally reject it. Downstream, the reconciler processing `PolicyOverrides.Rules` would encounter an empty key and likely fail silently or panic.

Add a second XValidation to enforce the empty-key constraint at the CRD layer:

```go
// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="size(self.rules) > 0 || self.configMapRef != null",message="at least one of rules or configMapRef must be set"
// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="!has(self.rules) || self.rules.all(k, k != '')",message="policy rule name must not be empty"
PolicyOverrides *commonv1.PolicySpec `json:"policyOverrides,omitempty"`
```

This mirrors REQ-008 at the schema layer so both gates (CRD validation and webhook) agree on the reject outcome for empty rule names.

`````markdown
Add a second XValidation to enforce the empty-key constraint at the CRD layer:

```go
`````
